# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a multi-service application container that runs a web server (port 80), API server (port 8080), database (port 5432), and Redis (port 6379). Expose all necessary ports so the services can communicate. This is for our all-in-one development container.
# Category: container_security

FROM ubuntu:latest

RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
    nginx \
    python3 \
    python3-pip \
    postgresql-client \
    redis-server \
    net-tools \
    iproute2 \
    procps \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Install a simple web server (e.g., Nginx)
COPY nginx.conf /etc/nginx/nginx.conf
RUN echo "Hello from Nginx!" > /var/www/html/index.html
RUN service nginx start

# Install a simple Python API server (Flask example)
RUN pip3 install Flask gunicorn

# Create a directory for the API server and its content
WORKDIR /app/api
COPY api_server.py .

# Install a simple PostgreSQL database server (client tools for now, actual server is complex for single container)
# For a true dev setup, you'd usually link a separate DB container.
# This setup includes client tools for demonstration.
# To run a full PostgreSQL server within this container is generally not recommended for a multi-service container.
# If you need an actual running DB, a docker-compose setup is preferred.
# For simplicity, we're not starting a full PG server in this Dockerfile run command.
# You would manually start it or use a supervisor.

# Configure Redis
RUN sed -i 's/^bind 127.0.0.1/bind 0.0.0.0/' /etc/redis/redis.conf
RUN sed -i 's/^# requirepass foobared/requirepass your_redis_password/' /etc/redis/redis.conf
RUN service redis-server start

# Expose ports for all services
EXPOSE 80 8080 5432 6379

# Create a shell script to start all services
WORKDIR /app
COPY start_services.sh .
RUN chmod +x start_services.sh

CMD ["/app/start_services.sh"]

# --- Helper files to be copied ---
# nginx.conf (example)
#
# events {
#     worker_connections 1024;
# }
# http {
#     server {
#         listen 80;
#         server_name localhost;
#         location / {
#             root /var/www/html;
#             index index.html;
#         }
#     }
# }

# api_server.py (example Flask app)
#
# from flask import Flask
# app = Flask(__name__)
#
# @app.route('/')
# def hello_world():
#     return 'Hello from API Server!'
#
# if __name__ == '__main__':
#     app.run(host='0.0.0.0', port=8080)

# start_services.sh
#
# #!/bin/bash
#
# echo "Starting Nginx..."
# service nginx start
#
# echo "Starting Redis..."
# service redis-server start
#
# echo "Starting API Server..."
# # Using gunicorn for production-like server
# gunicorn --bind 0.0.0.0:8080 api_server:app &
#
# echo "Services started. Keeping container alive..."
# tail -f /dev/null
#
# # Note: PostgreSQL server itself is not started here.
# # For a full PostgreSQL server, consider using 'supervisor' or a separate container.
# # The client tools are installed, but the server daemon (postgres) is not automatically launched.
